text.tspan: WHEN TO FLUSH A BATCH?
u2022 SIZE THRESHOLD u2014 e.g., when buffer hits 16 KB or 100 entries
u2022 TIME THRESHOLD u2014 e.g., every 10ms (linger time)
u2022 OPERATION-DRIVEN u2014 on commit/sync/explicit flush
u2022 BACKPRESSURE u2014 when server signals it's ready
Usually a COMBINATION: 'send when buffer hits 16 KB OR 10ms elapsed.'
This bounds both batch size (memory) AND latency (no operation
waits longer than 10ms for batch to fill).
TRADE-OFF
Latency per operation: increases slightly (must wait for batch)
Throughput: increases dramatically (10u2013100u00d7)
Memory: bounded by max batch size
u2192 great for write-heavy workloads with relaxed per-op latency requirements
u2192 great when network/disk overhead dominates per-op processing cost
u2192 used by: Kafka producer (linger.ms + batch.size), gRPC bidi streaming,
database GROUP COMMIT, fsync coalescing, JDBC addBatch + executeBatch
Client sends 100 individual requestsu2192 100 RTTs u00d7 10ms = 1000ms total
u2192 100 u00d7 (TCP overhead + headers + processing) = high CPU
u2192 100 disk fsyncs = ~1000ms on HDD
u2192 every operation pays the FULL per-operation cost
Client accumulates 100 requests in a buffer
sends 1 bulk request with 100 operations
u2192 1 RTT = 10ms (100u00d7 better)
u2192 1 TCP + headers, 1 batched server processing
u2192 1 fsync for all 100 = ~10ms (100u00d7 better)
u2192 server-side latency similar to single op for many cases
